text:
Hank had always felt a bit left out. He wasn’t exceptionally good-looking, wasn’t incredibly intelligent, wasn’t extraordinarily skilled at sports—nothing seemed to give him an edge. Most people seemed to gravitate towards him for some reason he couldn’t understand. It wasn’t till he stumbled upon a group of ducks that he realized why.

They were walking together, a flock of glossy wild ducks. Hank watched, utterly captivated. There was something about their graceful motion, their harmony. They wove through the water as if every step was music.

“Hey!” Hank finally hollered. The ducks flapped their wings in surprise and took flight, doing a little duck dance. “Hey!”

One of the ducks turned towards him. It had glossy green feathers and a bright red bill. “Hello,” it quacked softly. “You must be new here. Care to join us?”

“Well…” Hank hesitated. “I’ve never actually talked to someone like you.”

The duck
